title: DASHBOARD-DEPTH-2
scope: Phase 55 DASHBOARD-DEPTH + Phase 50/73 landlord-composable-dashboard sequel. The scout confirmed the depth-2 surface is genuinely unbuilt: custom dashboards (landlord_dashboards + DashboardService::buildPayload + Dashboards/{Index,Editor,Show}) support only two HARD-CODED card types (saved_report, metric), have NO share permalink, NO PDF/XLSX export, and the main role dashboard's cross-building rollup is a query-time toggle with no persistence. Reusable seams are READY: the Phase-73 report-share signed-URL pattern (ReportShareController + report_shares + share.blade.php), barryvdh/laravel-dompdf (InvoicePdfService pattern), and XlsxExportService::writeMultiSheet. This cycle deepens the COMPOSABLE dashboards: a card-renderer registry (so new card types are safe + ownership-validated), three new card types (kpi/chart/text), dashboard share permalinks, dashboard PDF+XLSX export, and a persisted cross-building scope. 6 categories x 3 = 18 (5H/9M/4L). Hard constraints preserved: DashboardService re-validates EVERY card's landlord ownership at render (fail-closed); shares run with the share row's landlord_id never a request param; no DB::raw; MetricFormulaService no-eval untouched.

findings.title: Extract a card-renderer registry from DashboardService's hard-coded type switch
findings.evidence: DashboardService::renderCard() hard-codes the only two card types (saved_report, metric) with an inline if/else; adding a type means editing the security-sensitive render path. There is no factory/registry, so each new type risks the per-card ownership-validation invariant.
findings.remediation: Introduce a DashboardCardRenderer interface (type(): string; validate(array $card, int $landlordId): array; render(array $card, int $landlordId): array) + a DashboardCardRegistry keyed by type. Extract the existing saved_report + metric logic VERBATIM into SavedReportCardRenderer + MetricCardRenderer (preserving requireSavedReport/requireMetric ownership checks, fail-closed). DashboardService::renderCard + validateLayout delegate to the registry (unknown type still throws ValidationException). Behaviour-preserving — Phase73DashboardEditorTest + Phase50 dashboard tests must still pass unchanged. Phase74 test: registry resolves both renderers; unknown type rejected.

findings.title: Register renderers via the container + allowed-types contract surfaced to the editor
findings.evidence: The editor (Dashboards/Editor.vue create()) hard-codes the two card-type buttons; there is no single source of truth for which types exist.
findings.remediation: Bind DashboardCardRegistry as a singleton in a service provider, registering the renderers. DashboardController::create/edit emit the registry's allowed card-type descriptors (key, label, needs_saved_report, needs_metric) so the editor renders the add-card buttons dynamically. Phase74 test asserts the create payload lists the registered types.

findings.title: Registry docs + card-type metrics gauge
findings.evidence: No operator doc for adding a card type; no visibility into render failures per type.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/reports.md gains a 'Dashboard card types' section (the renderer contract + how to add one). report_render_failure_count increment already exists in buildPayload; add a {card_type} label so failures attribute per type. Phase74 surface asserts the doc section + the registry class list.

findings.title: KPI tile card (single big-number metric, no table)
findings.evidence: The metric card always shows count + average; there is no compact single-value KPI tile (the most-wanted dashboard primitive).
findings.remediation: KpiCardRenderer (type='kpi'): bound to a metric_slug + saved_report_id like metric, but renders ONE aggregate (configurable agg: sum|avg|min|max|count over the metric values), returns {value, unit, label, agg}. Reuses MetricFormulaService::evaluate per row then aggregates. Ownership-validated via the registry. KpiCard.vue tile. Phase74 test: kpi card computes the chosen aggregate; cross-tenant metric_slug rejected.

findings.title: Chart card (bar chart from a saved report's label+value columns)
findings.evidence: Cards can only show tables or a metric number; landlords want a visual chart. No chart dependency is needed (the app already hand-rolls SVG/div bar charts in ReportsTab).
findings.remediation: ChartCardRenderer (type='chart'): bound to saved_report_id + a label_field + value_field (both validated against the report's returned columns); returns up to N (cap 50) {label, value} points. ChartCard.vue renders a div/SVG bar chart (no new dependency, matches ReportsTab style). Fail-closed if the fields aren't in the report output. Phase74 test: chart card returns capped points; unknown field rejected.

findings.title: Text/note card (static markdown-ish copy)
findings.evidence: No way to add a heading/explanatory note between data cards.
findings.remediation: TextCardRenderer (type='text'): no data source, just a sanitized title + body (length-capped, HTML-escaped at render). TextCard.vue. Validation requires non-empty body. Phase74 test: text card persists + renders escaped; oversize body rejected.

findings.title: DashboardShare model + signed public view (reuse the report-share pattern)
findings.evidence: Phase 73 shipped report-share signed URLs but dashboards have no shareable link; a landlord cannot send a read-only dashboard to an accountant/owner.
findings.remediation: Mirror ReportShare EXACTLY: dashboard_shares table (landlord_id, landlord_dashboard_id, expires_at, revoked_at?, last_viewed_at?, view_count) + DashboardShare model (TenantScope, isActive()). DashboardShareController index/store/revoke/view. Public view route is signed-gated (no auth), loads the dashboard withoutGlobalScope by share.landlord_dashboard_id+share.landlord_id (NEVER a request param), runs DashboardService::buildPayload with the share's landlord_id, renders a standalone Blade. Config drift / build failure degrades to 'unavailable' (no 500 to the public). Phase74 test: valid signature renders; tampered/expired/revoked 403; id cannot be swapped; cross-tenant store rejected.

findings.title: Share management UI + access trail
findings.evidence: Minted shares need listing, copy, revoke, and a view count.
findings.remediation: Dashboards/Shares.vue (mirror Reports/Shares.vue): pick a dashboard + expiry (1/7/30 days), list active/revoked with copy + revoke + view_count. view() bumps view_count + last_viewed_at + a report_share_views_count{surface=dashboard} gauge BEFORE render. Idempotent revoke. routes dashboards.shares.index/store/revoke + dashboards.share.view (public signed). Phase74 test: revoke is idempotent; foreign share 404.

findings.title: Share Blade view + lang
findings.evidence: The public dashboard view needs a standalone, escaped, no-auth template + localised UI copy.
findings.remediation: resources/views/dashboards/share.blade.php (standalone escaped HTML, renders cards by type, handles a $failed unavailable state). lang reports.dashboard_share.* (en full; sw/ar [TODO]). Phase74 surface asserts the Blade file + lang keys.

findings.title: Dashboard PDF export (dompdf, reuse the InvoicePdfService pattern)
findings.evidence: barryvdh/laravel-dompdf is installed and InvoicePdfService demonstrates the pattern, but a landlord cannot export a dashboard to PDF for an owner/board pack.
findings.remediation: DashboardPdfService::render(LandlordDashboard): build the payload via DashboardService::buildPayload, Pdf::loadView('dashboards.pdf', ...)->setPaper('a4','landscape'). dashboards.pdf Blade renders cards (saved_report tables, metric/kpi tiles, chart as a static table fallback). DashboardController::exportPdf streams the download (landlord-owned only). throttle:pdf-render. Phase74 test: owner gets a application/pdf stream; cross-tenant 404.

findings.title: Dashboard XLSX export (one sheet per data card)
findings.evidence: No spreadsheet export of a dashboard's underlying data.
findings.remediation: DashboardController::exportXlsx → flatten each saved_report/chart card into a sheet (title=card title, columns from row keys), call XlsxExportService::writeMultiSheet, stream. Metric/kpi cards become a single-row summary sheet. Owner-only. Phase74 test: returns the xlsx mime + a sheet per data card.

findings.title: Export buttons + lang on the dashboard show page
findings.evidence: The export endpoints need UI affordances.
findings.remediation: Dashboards/Show.vue gains PDF + XLSX export buttons (window.location to the routes) + an export dropdown. lang reports.dashboards.export_pdf/export_xlsx. Phase74 surface asserts the routes + lang keys + Show.vue tokens.

findings.title: Persist the main dashboard's all-buildings scope (currently query-time only)
findings.evidence: DashboardService::getCrossBuildingMetricsContext exists but the all-buildings view is only reachable via a ?building_id=all query param — the preference is not persisted, so the landlord re-toggles every visit.
findings.remediation: Persist a dashboard_scope preference ('active_building' | 'all_buildings') on the landlord's main_dashboard LandlordDashboard row (reuse DashboardPreferenceController/the existing main_dashboard slug row; store under a scope key, NOT a new table). DashboardController::landlord reads it (query param still overrides for one-off). DashboardPreferenceController::updateScope. Phase74 test: setting all_buildings persists + the next dashboard load aggregates across buildings; query param overrides.

findings.title: Scope toggle UI on the main dashboard
findings.evidence: No persistent UI control to switch active-building vs all-buildings.
findings.remediation: Dashboard.vue gains a scope toggle (Active building / All buildings) that POSTs the preference then reloads; reflects the persisted value on mount. Hidden for single-building landlords. lang dashboard.scope.*. Phase74 surface asserts the route + lang keys + Dashboard.vue token.

findings.title: All-buildings indicator + observability
findings.evidence: When aggregated, the user should see the scope; ops should see adoption.
findings.remediation: A small 'All buildings' badge in the dashboard header when aggregated. dashboard:scope-rollup not needed — instead a dashboard_all_buildings_landlords gauge emitted by an existing daily rollup (or a tiny counter on toggle). Phase74 surface asserts the gauge name in alert/metrics docs (visibility-only).

findings.title: Phase74 cross-category surface + isolation/share behaviour suite
findings.evidence: The card registry, new card types, dashboard shares, exports, and cross-building scope are all security-sensitive (cross-tenant + public-share + ownership validation) and must be guarded against drift.
findings.remediation: Phase74DashboardDepth2SurfaceTest: tables (dashboard_shares) + columns + classes (DashboardCardRegistry + 5 renderers + DashboardShare + DashboardShareController + DashboardPdfService) + routes (dashboards.shares.*, dashboards.share.view, dashboards.export-pdf, dashboards.export-xlsx, dashboard.scope.update) + Vue tokens (KpiCard/ChartCard/TextCard/Shares/export buttons) + lang parity. Behaviour: card-registry resolves/rejects, each new card type renders + cross-tenant-rejects, dashboard-share signature valid/tampered/expired/revoked + id-swap, export owner-only, scope persists. All RefreshDatabase + actingAs.

findings.title: Reuse-not-rebuild verification + InertiaPageReachability stays green
findings.evidence: Phase 74 deliberately reuses the report-share signed-URL + dompdf + XlsxExportService seams and adds new Inertia pages (Dashboards/Shares); the reachability guard must stay green.
findings.remediation: Assert DashboardService::buildPayload remains the single render path for show + share + pdf + xlsx (no duplicate card logic); assert InertiaPageReachabilityTest still passes (new Dashboards/Shares.vue exists). Confirm DashboardShareController mirrors ReportShareController's signed-gate (share.landlord_id, never a param).

findings.title: Runbook + nav + lang closeout
findings.evidence: New surfaces need operator docs + navigation + localisation.
findings.remediation: docs/runbooks/reports.md gains Dashboard-share + Dashboard-export + Card-types + Cross-building-scope sections. The report-tool launcher (ReportsTab) / Dashboards index gains a Share entry. lang parity finalised (en full; sw/ar [TODO]). Phase74 surface asserts the runbook sections + lang keys.

closeout.summary: Phase 50/73 composable-dashboard sequel. CARD-REGISTRY: extracted DashboardService's hard-coded card if/else into a DashboardCardRenderer registry (ownership guards lifted verbatim, fail-closed on unknown type). CARD-TYPES: kpi (aggregate tile), chart (bar from label+value, fail-closed on unknown field, 50-pt cap), text (escaped note). DASH-SHARE: dashboard_shares + signed public view (runs with share.landlord_id, never a param) mirroring report-share. DASH-EXPORT: dompdf + XlsxExportService multi-sheet, owner-only, sheet-title char sanitisation. CROSS-BUILDING: persisted main-dashboard scope (active_building|all_buildings) on the main_dashboard pref row {widgets,scope}, query-param override, multi-building gate.
closeout.tests: Phase74CardRegistryTest (5) + Phase74CardTypesTest (8) + Phase74DashboardShareTest (9) + Phase74DashboardExportTest (5) + Phase74CrossBuildingTest (6) + Phase74DashboardDepth2SurfaceTest (9). InertiaPageReachability + UiArchetype guards stay green.
closeout.constraints_preserved: Renderers re-validate landlord ownership of every referenced report/metric (fail-closed); signed dashboard-share runs with the row landlord_id; NO DB::raw; MetricFormulaService no-eval untouched; buildPayload is the single render path for show + share + pdf + xlsx.
closeout.coderabbit: Clean per sub-phase. Caught: CARD-TYPES chart field-error key (fixed); DASH-EXPORT illegal-sheet-char 500 (HIGH, fixed + regression test); CROSS-BUILDING surfaced a pre-existing main_dashboard-row leak into the dashboards list (tied off).
